Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The standard process typically includes posting a job ad screening candidates conducting interviews making a hiring decision onboarding adding to payroll and closing the case The expected order is generally sequential

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The partial order means that the edges define precedence constraints So Post must happen before Screen Screen must happen before Decide Screen must happen before Interview Decide must happen before Onboard Onboard must happen before Payroll Payroll must happen before Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no constraint between Interview and Decide So Interview and Decide can happen in any order relative to each other as long as both happen after Screen

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Typically interviews happen before the hiring decision So having Decide not necessarily after Interview is an anomaly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The model allows a flow where after screening you could make the hiring decision immediately without interviews or you could do interviews after the decision That is problematic

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interview could be at any time after Screen even after Close That seems very odd

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So anomalies in Model 1 The hiring decision can be made without conducting interviews Interviews might be conducted after the hiring decision

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Loop Onboard skip means execute Onboard then either exit or go back to Onboard So effectively after Onboard you can either exit or go back to Onboard

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: That might represent multiple onboarding steps Possibly but typically onboarding is done once per hire

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: XOR Payroll skip means either do Payroll or skip So adding to payroll might be skipped

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Typically after hiring you must add the employee to payroll Skipping payroll would mean the employee does not get paid That is a severe issue

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post goes before Screen and Interview But there is no edge between Screen and Interview So Screen and Interview can happen in any order after Post

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Typically screening happens before interviews So having them unordered is a deviation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no edge from Screen to Decide So you could potentially make a decision without screening Since Interview goes to Decide Interview is mandatory but Screen is not

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So anomalies in Model 2 Screening can be skipped entirely Screening and interviews can be performed in any order Onboarding can be repeated multiple times Payroll addition can be skipped

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Normative process should have Screening before Interviews Interviews before Decision Onboarding once Payroll mandatory

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 ensures Screening and Payroll are mandatory but does not enforce Interviews before Decision Model 2 enforces Interviews before Decision but does not enforce Screening and Payroll

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Skipping interviews entirely is definitely a bad practice In Model 1 you can hire without interviews In Model 2 you can skip screening but still have interviews so at least there is some evaluation

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If we could fix one anomaly in each Model 1 would need an edge from Interview to Decide Model 2 would need to make Screen mandatory and remove the skip option from payroll

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I think Model 2 is closer because it at least has the correct order between Interview and Decide

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 2 ensures interviews are conducted before the hiring decision whereas Model 1 allows hiring without interviews which is a severe violation of hiring best practices
